description: This figure focuses on Fe-6.8Al ferritic low-density steel under three processing conditions: 98% cold rolled (98%CR), isothermally annealed at 750 °C for 5 min, and annealed at 750 °C for 60 min. Subplot (a) shows the normalized (110) XRD peak profiles, with inset presenting full XRD patterns confirming single BCC ferrite phase for all samples; the 98%CR sample has the broadest peak, which becomes sharper with increasing annealing time, indicating reduced lattice microstrain. Subplots (b), (c), (d) plot the second order moment M2 and fourth order moment M4/q² as functions of q for the three samples respectively, used for dislocation density calculation. Subplot (e) shows that dislocation density drops by 3 orders of magnitude from ~2.2×10^15 m⁻² for 98%CR sample to ~1×10^12 m⁻² after 60 min annealing, attributed to dislocation annihilation during recrystallization. The results support that recrystallization-induced dislocation density reduction is a key reason for strength decrease after annealing of cold rolled Fe-6.8Al steel.
